title: Chapter Forty-Nine: Dragon Phoenix Gathering
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The Namgung family's gate was massive, its pavilion complex pure and imposing. But the warriors guarding it wore strange expressions.

"Welcome. Have you come for the Dragon Phoenix Gathering?"

Yeon-shin clasped his fists. "I am Gu Gwa of the Chasing Truth Sect."

"Confirmed." The warrior examined the paper and nodded. "Though regrettable, the Gathering is not being held within the family compound. A major matter in the main house -- the location has moved. Please understand."

"Where?"

"Lotus Pavilion. North of Huizhou. I will send a guide."

*Something is wrong here.*

A prickling sensation spread from his crown. The energy unique to the Thirteen Heavens -- rough, ominous, unmistakable. He had felt it before against the Tyrant Sword Sect. Against Blood Flame.

Upper dantian foresight. Intuition sharpening into certainty.

*The Namgung family has been attacked. They are hiding it.*

He followed the guide without showing what he sensed. The walk was long -- far enough that horses would have been faster. The city streets of Huizhou were broad and paved smooth, merchant groups and tea traders jostling past with their enormous bundles, but the faces of common folk were not all good. Whispers of poor harvests. Tight smiles over tight purses.

Shin So-bin whispered beside him about the strangeness of the Namgung family's behavior. He let her words pass. His mind was turning over the implications.

The Dragon Phoenix Gathering, forced outside the family compound. No elders present. Rising talents gathered in the open, without protection.

*Families are clans in the secular world. They have enough power to drive away adults. The unsound Namgung family. Young people's deviation suppressed by family.*

Bait.

---

The sound of the Lotus Pavilion reached them before the sight.

*Boom!*

Waves rose from the lakeside. White foam caught the sun and fell like shattered glass.

Under the huge pavilion built above the water, two figures clashed. A handsome young Taoist in Mount Hua robes. And opposite him -- the Lazy Flame Dragon, sword drawn, eyes blazing with mad crimson light.

Different from the road.

Utterly different.

Where Yeon-shin had faced a languid, amused man, this was something else entirely. Madness radiated from the Lazy Flame Dragon's smile. An appearance that had thrown away everything -- family, reputation, restraint.

"Lecturing me with pathetic talent?" the young master roared, his true energy waves hammering the air. "What could you accomplish if you were me! How futile!"

Yu Hyeon -- the Mount Hua Sect leader's chief disciple, the one Yeon-shin had helped with the Great Violet Pill -- was bleeding. Sword cuts through his Taoist robes. His face was crumpled with something close to terror as he staggered backward.

Rising talents watched from the pavilion and its roof. Over thirty of them. Young men and women from sects across the realm -- family young masters, Taoists, even monks. Those sitting with feet on railings. Those standing tall on the pavilion's curved roof. Their faces taut with fear.

It was a very unfamiliar sight to Yeon-shin. Having repeatedly fought bloody battles for missions, it was his first time seeing young and junior disciples from various sects gathered in one place. Seeing several peers felt strange. Like looking into a mirror that showed a life he might have lived, if things had been different.

No one dared intervene. Even Hwangbo Myeong-rin and her brother stood frozen at the edge.

"What to do about that!"

"Sir Baek went to call people!"

Yeon-shin stepped through the crowd.

The rising talents' eyes widened at his passage, but by then he was already at the Lazy Flame Dragon's side.

"Why you again!" Killing intent erupted from bloodshot eyes. "Still insignificant!"

*Bang!*

Yu Hyeon went flying from a final strike. Simultaneously, the Lazy Flame Dragon's hand descended toward Yeon-shin's skull -- no mercy, no restraint, a blow backed by the full weight of scorching yang energy.

*Crash!*

Not force wave against force wave. Something different. Yeon-shin's hand traced a strange arc -- forearm pushing forearm, wrist rolling down. In an instant he spread thumb and fingers wide and wedged them into the inside of the Lazy Flame Dragon's arm joint.

Grip. True energy layered with Fate Defying Scripture pouring into the hold.

He pressed the inner gate point where the arm bent, flooding it with internal energy. Acupoint sealed. Strength drained from the limb in a heartbeat.

The power remained the same. He had surpassed in technique.

Stillness.

"What."

The Lazy Flame Dragon's expression went blank. They stood close enough that Yeon-shin could feel the heat radiating from the man's skin -- feverish, unnatural, the fire of a body consuming itself.

"You were talking about talent," Yeon-shin said. "What about yourself?"

"What?"

He had dug in close -- a distance too tight even for grappling. He looked into the man's shadowed eyes and spoke quietly.

"You are a bit duller."

---

Whispers spread through the Lotus Pavilion like wind through tall grass.

"Lazy Flame Dragon's acupoints have been sealed."

"Techniques I have never seen before. Body technique, grappling, acupoint sealing -- all of it."

"Which sect is he from?"

Yeon-shin released his grip but did not unseal the acupoints.

*He will unseal them himself when he circulates his energy. Yang energy disperses foreign power easily.*

Lazy Flame Dragon stared at him. The madness was draining from his eyes like water from a cracked vessel, replaced by something lucid and strange.

"I am more dull? Dull, you say?"

"If you cannot feel that much, that is all there is to it."

A calm anger. The Lazy Flame Dragon chewed and swallowed a winter cherry whole, then spoke.

"Gu Gwa of the Chasing Truth Sect. I understood your talent with that one move. Someone like you will be talked about for decades. Even after death."

"And?"

*Cheong Myeong's lesson. The ultimate provocation is to mock someone's intimate stories. Stay quiet. Let him speak.*

"Do you know the feeling when your feet naturally move toward the abyss? You have everything. Your complexion is excellent. How does it feel to have talent that requires no price?"

The question struck something inside Yeon-shin that he had not expected.

*What?*

"I was born with extreme yang energy," the Lazy Flame Dragon said, his fingertips trembling. "I grew stronger without practicing. My heightened senses were accompanied by burning pain in my meridians. That is my achievement. Martial arts built while trying to change my constitution through inner power training. It is not something you can dismiss as being dull."

*He is confessing to a stranger. As if drinking with a close friend.*

It was embarrassing. Welcome for the mission, but strange -- a character type Yeon-shin had never encountered.

"What am I supposed to do about you being like that," Yeon-shin said. "I do not possess martial arts impressive enough to overwhelm such talent."

"You really--"

"I called what was dull, dull. There is nothing wrong with that. Only you know your pain. No one else can understand it."

He had ended up talking about himself.

Silence.

"Only I know?" The Lazy Flame Dragon's lips trembled. "Never heard that before. Everyone said they understood."

*This is not working.*

Every insult landed as intimacy. Every provocation drew the man closer rather than pushing him away. The madness of long suffering had inverted all normal reactions.

"If I were you," Yeon-shin tried, "I would have cut ties with the family and devoted myself to Desolate Sect. The fruit of the World Tree cures all ailments. The same would be true for Solar Divine Meridian. To discuss life without that much effort is laughable."

"Ha!" Genuine laughter. "You are worried about me!"

"I said you were even more dull."

"Where are you going? Let us drink together."

"Your worthless talent would spoil the wine. I do not share tables with insignificant people."

More laughter. Hearty, free, rolling across the pavilion.

*Completely mad.*

Yeon-shin turned away. He found Yu Hyeon getting to his feet alone in the distance, battered and bloodied.

"Where are the elders who accompanied you?"

"They decided to send everyone out from inside the Gathering," Yu Hyeon said, wincing. "How could we comfortably socialize with elders watching. Anyway -- thanks. Indeed..." His eyes moved, catching the covered Desolate Sect mark beneath Yeon-shin's collar. Sharp as ever. "...Gu Gwa."

"You are not angry. Even though I interrupted your contest."

"It should have ended earlier. The levels did not match." Yu Hyeon paused. His expression became complicated as he registered the blue of Yeon-shin's uniform beneath the robe. "You have become incredibly strong. It feels like a completely different level."

*We live in different times.*

Yeon-shin swallowed the words. Rising talents were approaching now -- young masters and disciples from sects he had never met, drawn by the spectacle.

"I have never seen anyone speak so harshly to Lazy Flame Dragon. I am Wei Wu-ling of the Jangbaek Sect."

"Yin Li-ming of the White Blade Sect. Your techniques were quite creative."

"If the Chasing Truth Sect has a rising talent like you, they will soon soar."

They gathered one by one. Yeon-shin returned their greetings while sensing their energy -- each one a system of martial arts, each spirit a piece of inspiration for the internal cultivation technique he was building.

From across the pavilion, Hwangbo Myeong-rin glared with murder in her eyes.

Good.

---
